marks_distribution: Unit 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry - 7 Marks
Unit 3: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties - 6 Marks
Unit 4: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure - 7 Marks
Unit 8: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques - 11 Marks
question_paper_design: Formative Assessment Topics (No Summative Weightage): s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State
competency_weightage: Atomic Models and Quantum Mechanical Model
Chemical Bonding Theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital)
Thermodynamic Terms, Enthalpy Change, Spontaneity, Gibbs Energy
Equilibrium Constants, Reaction Quotient, Factors Affecting Equilibria
bloom_levels: Knowledge: Laws of Chemical Combination, Atomic Theory, Periodic Law
Understanding: Bohr's Model, VSEPR Theory, Bonding Types
Application: Stoichiometric Calculations, pH Determination, Titration
Analysis: Electronic Configuration Trends, Equilibrium Shifts
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment for s & p Block Elements (No marks in final theory exam)
Formative Assessment for The Gaseous State (No marks in final theory exam)

syllabus_areas: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing, Boring)
Experiments based on pH (Fruit juices, Strong/Weak acids, Titration)
Quantitative Estimation (Balance usage, Standard solutions, Titrations)

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources
suggested_topics: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water (sulphide ion)
Testing hardness/ions in regional drinking water
Rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers
Acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
project_assessment.note: Projects should involve about 10 periods of work with teacher approval
